evidence.evidence_text: Early in the movie dialogue, Pando is introduced as Crowbar Jones's sidekick: "I couldn't have done it without my trusty sidekick, Pando." Another line says, "Classic Pando."
The movie itself portrays Pando as comic relief. Dialogue around 56.95s–86.19s says, "Dude, everyone is laughing at Pando" and "He's the comedy relief."
Later movie dialogue depicts Pando as struggling: "Crowbar, help me!" "I think something's wrong with my jetpack!" and Crowbar responds, "Get it together, Pando."

The movie also jokes about Pando's swimming and phone use: "If only I knew how to swim!" followed by "That's not true! I do know how to swim!" and later a viewer complains, "I don't spend all my time on the phone, I'm not clumsy, and I do know how to swim."

A viewer explicitly comments on the portrayal: "Pando is a huge misrepresentation of who I am." This confirms later complaints are about how the movie depicts Pando, not neutral narration of the real person.
The queried in-movie Pando/Panda character is visually grounded in intervals 22.00s-35.00s, 56.00s-59.00s, 67.00s-76.00s, and 149.00s-162.00s.

Across the retrieved frames that show Panda/Pando (described as frames 12-16 and 19-22 in the visual review), Panda is repeatedly shown sitting next to Grizzly or sitting alone, with standard black-and-white fur. The review explicitly notes no glasses and no bow tie on Panda in frame 12, frame 13, frame 14, frame 15, frame 16, and frames 19-22.
